documentation.readme: Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .
usage_sections: Quick Start: > Please see the Wiki for complete documentation. Havoc works well on Debian 10/11, Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 and Kali Linux. It's recommended to use the latest versions possible to avoid issues. You'll need a modern version of Qt and Python 3.10.x to avoid build issues. See the Install

Features: Client > Cross-platform UI written in C++ and Qt - Modern, dark theme based on Dracula Teamserver > Written in Golang - Multiplayer - Payload generation (exe/shellcode/dll) - HTTP/HTTPS listeners - Customizable C2 profiles - External C2 Demon > Havoc's flagship agent written in C

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the frameworks category. Fits when the engagement needs: Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `Havoc` from its upstream repository, then run `Havoc --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .

name: Havoc
summary: Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need frameworks, use Havoc because Havoc is a modern and malleable post-exploitation command and control framework, created by @C5pider .
When chaining frameworks tools, pair Havoc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Havoc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
